title: × Ammocalamagrostis baltica
intro: × Ammocalamagrostis baltica, also known as Baltic bentgrass, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the grass family (Poaceae). The Baltic bentgrass is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The Baltic bentgrass is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in the production of herbal supplements and extracts.
taxonomy: × Ammocalamagrostis baltica is a nothospecies, which is a hybrid between two different species of plants. Specifically, it is a cross between Ammophila baltica and Calamagrostis arundinacea. This classification indicates that it is a man-made hybrid, created to combine the desirable traits of its parent species. The classification of nothospecies is used to distinguish hybrids that are stable and reproduce true-to-type, but are not yet recognized as a distinct species. As a result, × Ammocalamagrostis baltica is often used in research and cultivation to study the characteristics of its parent species.
